title: Animation libraries for Next.js/React
corePrinciple: The library you choose matters less than how you use it. Architecture (composition, lazy-loading, GPU constraints) beats bundle size and feature count.

libraries.name: Motion
libraries.what: Declarative React component animation, gestures, exit transitions
libraries.key: AnimatePresence enables exit animations before unmount

libraries.name: GSAP
libraries.what: Timeline orchestration for complex multi-element sequences and scroll effects
libraries.license: Free core; premium plugins need Club GSAP. Prohibits use in Webflow competitors.
libraries.key: Use timeline model for precise inter-element timing
libraries.notes: Strip dev console logs before prod — costs per frame. prefers-reduced-motion via matchMedia.

libraries.name: React Spring
libraries.what: Physics-based spring animation, not duration tweens

libraries.name: Anime.js
libraries.key: Best for per-character text stagger and grid animations

libraries.name: Three.js
libraries.key: Direct ref mutation in useFrame, never React state

libraries.name: React Three Fiber (R3F)
libraries.what: React renderer for Three.js (3D in JSX)
libraries.key: NEVER setState inside useFrame — mutate refs directly (avoids re-render/frame drops)
libraries.notes: Dynamic import ssr:false to keep off server bundle

libraries.name: Lenis
libraries.what: Inertia smooth scroll, lerp interpolation, GSAP ScrollTrigger integration
avoid: data-heavy dashboards / functional UIs where scroll speed matters
libraries.setup: ReactLenis wrapper + useLenis(ScrollTrigger.update) for GSAP sync

libraries.name: LottieFiles / DotLottie
libraries.what: Plays JSON vector animations from After Effects; DotLottie compresses ~80%
libraries.key: Prefer .lottie over .json for much smaller files

libraries.name: Rive
libraries.what: State-machine interactive animation; responds to runtime inputs/logic
libraries.key: State machine model vs Lottie's pre-recorded timeline

libraries.name: Aceternity UI
libraries.what: Prebuilt animated components, theatrical (beam bg, card tilt, spotlight cursor)

libraries.name: Magic UI
libraries.what: Copy-paste animated components, micro-interactions over theatrics

libraries.name: React Bits
libraries.what: Animated component kit, CSS-based, many RSC-compatible
libraries.advantage: Many components run in Server Components (no 'use client') unlike Aceternity/Magic UI

decisionFramework: 8. Prebuilt animation file? -> LottieFiles (timeline) or Rive (state machine)
9. Copy-paste component? -> Aceternity / Magic UI / React Bits

stack: Motion for React UI (modals, drawers, page/component transitions)
GSAP + ScrollTrigger for marketing sequences (choreographed/pinned reveals)
Three.js/R3F for 3D (only when it earns the payload; dynamic import ssr:false)
Rive for interactive state (animations that respond to user input+logic)
Component kits selectively (1-2 per section max)
CSS for everything else (hover, focus, simple transitions)

productionRules: Only animate transform and opacity (GPU compositing; avoid layout-triggering props)
Lazy-load heavy libs via next/dynamic ssr:false (Three.js, Lottie, Rive)
Never setState inside useFrame (R3F) — mutate refs directly
Respect prefers-reduced-motion (Motion auto; GSAP via matchMedia)
Test on real mid-range mobile hardware, not emulators
Strip dev logs before prod (GSAP logs cost per-frame processing)
